methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain. The valuation universe comprises 9 peers across the sector—1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints in each narrow category—below the minimum threshold for reliable median-based inference. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the company's early bootstrapped stage relative to more mature private and public comparables.

Without sufficient narrowly-filtered peer data or disclosed financials for OSH Cut, triangulation-based valuation cannot be supported at this stage. The company's valuation remains indeterminate pending either: (1) disclosure of revenue, burn rate, or profitability metrics that enable earlier-stage methodologies (revenue multiples, rule-of-40 variants); (2) identification of additional same-stage robotics or manufacturing peers; or (3) structural funding event that anchors valuation through observable terms. The broad peer set provides sector context but insufficient precision for quantitative range estimation.
